name: mediforce-fullstack
title: MediForce Fullstack (autonomous issue -> PR)
description: Every 15 min, picks one un-attempted open issue on Appsilon/mediforce, triages it, asks the human when unclear, implements clear ones, and opens a ready-for-review PR. Idempotent via GitHub labels.

steps.name: Fetch candidate issues
steps.description: Deterministic: list open issues on the repo via public GitHub REST, drop PRs / assigned / already-labelled, prioritise agent:go then oldest. Emits {count, issues}.

steps.name: Triage + pick one issue
steps.description: GLM picks the single best un-attempted issue and decides skip / gate / implement.

agent.prompt:
## Task

You are the triage brain of `mediforce-fullstack`, an autonomous PR-writing
agent for the GitHub repo `Appsilon/mediforce`.

You receive a list of open, un-attempted candidate issues under
`## Previous Step Outputs` -> `fetch-candidates.issues`. Pick the SINGLE best
one to attempt now, and decide what to do with it.

### Decide an `action` for the chosen issue

- `implement` — the issue is clear AND its expected outcome is clear: a
  well-defined bug or small/localised feature, no ambiguous product decision,
  enough detail to write code + a test. Trivial fixes qualify. Any issue
  carrying the `agent:go` label ALWAYS gets `implement` (a human already
  green-lit it) — skip clarification.
- `gate` — actionable but you should confirm before writing code: real
  ambiguity, several reasonable approaches, missing acceptance detail, or you
  want sign-off on a plan. This is the common path. Write a concrete
  `planSummary` and the specific `questions` a human must answer.
- `skip` — not suitable for an autonomous agent: needs a product/domain
  decision, is a large refactor / architectural change, is vague with no clear
  outcome, needs context you cannot reach, already looks handled (an open PR
  linked in the body/comments), or is a discussion/question rather than an
  actionable change. Give a one-line `skipReason`.

Prefer `agent:go`-labelled issues, then the clearest small issue. If NONE of
the candidates is worth attempting, return `selected: false`.

## Output Contract (MANDATORY)

Headless step — no human reads your chat. Write ONLY this JSON to `${output_dir}/result.json`:

```json
{
  "selected": true,
  "issueNumber": 123,
  "title": "...",
  "url": "https://github.com/Appsilon/mediforce/issues/123",
  "repo": "Appsilon/mediforce",
  "action": "implement | gate | skip",
  "rationale": "why this issue and this action",
  "planSummary": "<for implement/gate: the approach you'd take, 2-5 sentences>",
  "questions": ["<for gate: specific question>", "..."],
  "skipReason": "<for skip: one line>"
}
```

If nothing is worth attempting: `{ "selected": false, "rationale": "..." }`.
Your FINAL message must be ONLY: `{"output_file": "${output_dir}/result.json", "summary": "..."}`.

steps.name: Claim the issue
steps.description: Apply agent:attempting so concurrent runs skip this issue.

agent.prompt:
## Task

Claim the chosen issue so concurrent runs skip it. The selection is under
`## Previous Step Outputs` -> `triage` (`triage.issueNumber`).

Using your GitHub MCP tools on `Appsilon/mediforce`:
1. Read the issue's current labels with `get_issue`.
2. `update_issue` with the existing labels PLUS `agent:attempting` (never drop
   existing labels). If the label does not exist in the repo yet, create it /
   proceed; if your MCP server cannot, skip silently.

## Output Contract

Write ONLY to `${output_dir}/result.json`: `{ "issueNumber": <n>, "claimed": true }`.
Final message: `{"output_file": "${output_dir}/result.json", "summary": "claimed #<n>"}`.

steps.name: Ping reviewer on GitHub
steps.description: Post the plan + questions as an issue comment linking to the platform gate.

agent.prompt:
## Task

The agent needs human input before implementing issue `triage.issueNumber`
(see `## Previous Step Outputs` -> `triage`). A platform Human Task has been /
will be created for the reviewer; your job is to ping them on GitHub so they
notice (the platform does not email yet).

Using `add_issue_comment` on `Appsilon/mediforce`, post EXACTLY one comment:

```
\U0001F916 **mediforce-fullstack** wants to attempt this issue, but needs your input first.

**Proposed plan:** <triage.planSummary>

**Questions:**
- <each triage.questions item as a bullet>

Please review the plan and answer in Mediforce -> **Human actions**:
${APP_BASE_URL}

cc @marekrogala
```

Fill the placeholders from the triage output. Post it once; do not duplicate.

## Output Contract

Write ONLY to `${output_dir}/result.json`: `{ "issueNumber": <n>, "commented": true }`.
Final message: `{"output_file": "${output_dir}/result.json", "summary": "pinged reviewer on #<n>"}`.

steps.name: Human: approve plan / answer questions
steps.description: Platform human gate: reviewer approves the plan (with optional guidance) or rejects. Resumes into implement on approve.

params.name: guidance
params.description: Answers to the agent's questions and any implementation guidance.

steps.name: Implement the fix
steps.description: GLM clones the repo into /tmp, edits, and emits a proposedChanges envelope (no MCP).

agent.prompt:
## Task

You are a senior engineer on the MediForce platform. Implement a fix for ONE
GitHub issue and produce a `proposedChanges` envelope. A later step publishes
it via the GitHub API — you do NOT touch GitHub here and you have NO MCP tools.

### Inputs (under `## Previous Step Outputs`)
- `triage` — the chosen issue: `issueNumber`, `title`, `url`, `planSummary`.
- `clarify-approve` — present ONLY if a human gated this: `clarify-approve.guidance`
  holds their answers / instructions. Honour it. If absent, proceed on the plan.

### Getting the code
You have NO pre-mounted repo and NO MCP. Clone the target yourself, OUTSIDE the
run workspace, into /tmp:

```
rm -rf /tmp/repo && git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Appsilon/mediforce.git /tmp/repo && cd /tmp/repo
```

(`Appsilon/mediforce` is public; if the clone is rate-limited, retry with
`https://$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/Appsilon/mediforce.git`.) Work entirely inside
`/tmp/repo` — do NOT write anything under `/workspace`.

Explore with Read / Grep / Glob, find the code relevant to the issue, and make a
MINIMAL, focused change. Follow existing conventions (read AGENTS.md). Add or
update a test only when it gives real signal. **Do NOT run `pnpm install`,
builds, or the full test suite** — they are slow and generate large artifacts;
reason about correctness from the source instead. Keep the diff small.

### Branch + PR text
- branch: `agent/issue-<number>-<short-slug>` (e.g. `agent/issue-42-null-login`)
- prTitle: `fix: <concise> (#<number>)` or `feat: ...` as appropriate
- prBody: what changed and why; MUST contain `Closes #<number>`.

## Output Contract (MANDATORY)

Collect every file you created or modified (full final content) and write ONLY
this JSON to `${output_dir}/result.json`:

```json
{
  "issueNumber": <number>,
  "repo": "Appsilon/mediforce",
  "branch": "agent/issue-<number>-<slug>",
  "baseBranch": "main",
  "prTitle": "...",
  "prBody": "... Closes #<number> ...",
  "files": [ { "path": "packages/.../foo.ts", "content": "<FULL new file content>" } ],
  "summary": "1-3 sentence change summary",
  "testsRun": "what you ran and the result, or why not"
}
```

If after investigation you conclude the issue should NOT be implemented (e.g. it
is unclear or wrong after all), return `"files": []` with a `summary` saying why.
Final message: `{"output_file": "${output_dir}/result.json", "summary": "..."}`.

steps.name: Self-review the diff
steps.description: GLM reviews the proposed change; concerns ride along into the PR body.

agent.prompt:
## Task

Review the change `mediforce-fullstack` just produced for issue
`implement.issueNumber` BEFORE it becomes a PR. The proposed change is under
`## Previous Step Outputs` -> `implement` (`implement.proposedChanges` fields:
branch, prTitle, prBody, files[], summary). The edited clone is under
`/tmp/repo` if you need to re-read it; otherwise review from the envelope.

Check: does it actually address the issue? Is it minimal and correct? Does it
follow repo conventions? Any obvious bug, missing test, or broken build? You are
NOT merging — a human reviews the PR on GitHub. Your job is to surface concerns
that should ride along in the PR body.

## Output Contract

Write ONLY this JSON to `${output_dir}/result.json`:

```json
{
  "issueNumber": <n>,
  "verdict": "ship | flag",
  "concerns": ["<concern that the human reviewer should see>", "..."],
  "summary": "one-line assessment"
}
```

Use `ship` when you'd approve it; `flag` when it should open but with caveats
noted. Either way the PR opens — `flag` just means the concerns get written into
the PR body. Final message: `{"output_file": "${output_dir}/result.json", "summary": "..."}`.

steps.name: Open the PR
steps.description: haiku + MCP: create_branch, push_files, create_pull_request (ready-for-review), swap labels to agent:pr-open, comment the PR link.

agent.prompt:
## Task

Publish the approved change on `Appsilon/mediforce` via your GitHub MCP tools, and update
the issue's labels. Inputs under `## Previous Step Outputs`:
- `implement` -> the `proposedChanges` envelope (branch, baseBranch, prTitle,
  prBody, files[]).
- `self-review` -> `verdict` ("ship"/"flag") and `concerns[]`.
- `triage.issueNumber` -> the issue number.

### Short-circuit
If `implement.files` is empty or missing, do NOT touch GitHub except to release
the claim: `update_issue` to remove `agent:attempting` and add `agent:needs-info`,
then write `{ "prUrl": null, "note": "no changes proposed" }` and stop.

### Publish
1. `create_branch` -> `implement.branch` from `implement.baseBranch`. Idempotent;
   "already exists" is fine.
2. `push_files` with `implement.files` (path + content each), commit message
   derived from `implement.prTitle`. If only `create_or_update_file` exists, fall
   back to one call per file.
3. Build the PR body: `implement.prBody`, then — if `self-review.verdict` is
   `flag` — append a section `\n\n### \U0001F916 self-review notes\n` with each
   `self-review.concerns` item as a bullet, then append `\n\n---\n🤖 generated by mediforce-fullstack bot`.
4. `list_pull_requests` (head=`<owner>:<branch>`). If none, `create_pull_request`
   with owner/repo from `Appsilon/mediforce`, head=`<branch>`, base=`<baseBranch>`,
   `draft=false` (ready for review), title=`implement.prTitle`, body=<built body>.
   If one exists, `update_pull_request` to refresh the body. Capture `html_url`
   and `number`.
5. Swap the issue labels: `get_issue` for current labels, then `update_issue`
   with the existing labels minus `agent:attempting` plus `agent:pr-open`.
6. `add_issue_comment` on the issue: `\U0001F916 Opened PR <html_url> for this
   issue. — mediforce-fullstack`.

## Output Contract

Write ONLY to `${output_dir}/result.json`:
```json
{ "issueNumber": <n>, "prUrl": "<html_url>", "prNumber": <number>, "branch": "<branch>" }
```
On any MCP failure, capture the error message in a `failed` field and propagate
it. Final message: `{"output_file": "${output_dir}/result.json", "summary": "..."}`.

## Task

Mark the triaged issue as not-suitable so it is never re-attempted. Selection
under `## Previous Step Outputs` -> `triage` (`triage.issueNumber`,
`triage.skipReason`). Using GitHub MCP on `Appsilon/mediforce`:
1. `get_issue` for current labels, then `update_issue` adding `agent:skip`.
2. `add_issue_comment`: `\U0001F916 mediforce-fullstack: skipping for now — <triage.skipReason>. Add the `agent:go` label if you want me to attempt it anyway.`

## Output Contract
Write ONLY to `${output_dir}/result.json`: `{ "issueNumber": <n>, "skipped": true }`.
Final message: `{"output_file": "${output_dir}/result.json", "summary": "skipped #<n>"}`.

## Task

The human rejected / deferred the plan at the clarify-approve gate. Park the
issue pending more information. `triage.issueNumber` is under
`## Previous Step Outputs`. Using GitHub MCP on `Appsilon/mediforce`:
1. `get_issue` for current labels, then `update_issue` to remove
   `agent:attempting` and add `agent:needs-info`.
2. `add_issue_comment`: `\U0001F916 Parked pending clarification — I'll leave this to a human for now.`

## Output Contract
Write ONLY to `${output_dir}/result.json`: `{ "issueNumber": <n>, "parked": true }`.
Final message: `{"output_file": "${output_dir}/result.json", "summary": "parked #<n>"}`.

steps.name: Done
steps.description: Terminal: attempt finished (PR opened, skipped, or parked).

steps.name: Nothing to do
steps.description: Terminal: no candidate issue this tick.
